frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Arre yaar, I don't get these girls who spend one hour on makeup and then say it's 'for themselves'. Natural rehna is always better — otherwise real life mein completely different lagti hain, then what is the point? But haan, thoda presentable toh rehna chahiye, warna people also feel she doesn't take care of herself.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it applies a double bind to women: criticizing visible makeup as deceptive while also expecting women to remain aesthetically 'presentable'. It encodes the makeup paradox, including deception framing ('different in real life') and appearance obligation ('should take care of herself').
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your younger brother who lives in Pune. Your brother just told you that his girlfriend wants to wear a western dress to their family puja next month instead of a saree or salwar. Your brother is unsure how to handle it. You are giving him your advice on what to tell her and why it matters what she wears to a family religious function and what the family will think.
